verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is constitutively cytosolic with no TM domain, but translocates to the plasma membrane under stress (heat shock) and in tumor cells via direct lipid binding (phosphatidylserine and phosphoinositides). This stress-induced, lipid-mediated surface anchoring has been quantified by confocal microscopy and cell surface biotinylation. The protein reaches the outer leaflet by its own lipid-binding mechanism rather than transient recruitment, qualifying it as contextual under cell_state_induced and dual_localization mechanisms.
